ogTitle: Lake Bennett vs Lambells Lagoon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Lake Bennett and Lambells Lagoon.
heading: Lake Bennett vs Lambells Lagoon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Lake Bennett, Northern Territory and Lambells Lagoon, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Lambells Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Lake Bennett (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Lake Bennett or Lambells Lagoon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Lake Bennett has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Lambells Lagoon scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Lake Bennett scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Lambells Lagoon scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Lake Bennett or Lambells Lagoon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Lambells Lagoon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
